subject: [PATCH v2 110/110] vfs: remove kino_t typedef and PRIino format macro

reviews.author: Jan Kara
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch removes the kino_t typedef and PRIino format macro, but did not explicitly request any changes or express disagreement.
reviews.raw_body:
Looks good. Feel free to add:

Reviewed-by: Jan Kara <jack@suse.cz>

								Honza

2026-03-03.patch_summary: This patch removes the `kino_t` typedef and the `PRIino` format macro from the Linux kernel, replacing them with `u64` type and format strings respectively. The changes are made to reflect that `i_ino` has been widened to a 64-bit unsigned integer. The patch updates various files in the kernel tree to use the new types and formats, removing the need for the typedef and macro.

2026-03-04.patch_summary: This patch removes the kino_t typedef and PRIino format macro from the Linux kernel, replacing them with u64 type and format strings respectively. The changes are made throughout the tree, including in various file system-related files such as fs/dcache.c, fs/eventpoll.c, and include/linux/fs.h. The removal of these macros is necessary because i_ino has been widened to a 64-bit unsigned integer (u64), making the typedef and macro redundant.
